shownotes: This show was believed to be held as a celebration for the 500th issue of Forte Magazine. The recording was seemingly distributed on cassette tape, of which only two copies are known to exist. The previously unknown song is believed to be Gypsy. Ghost is a cover of a song by Goodnight Owl (a.k.a. Love Migrate), a defunct band that included Joey and Eric. Lunch Meat was performed with the alternate arrangement heard on the demo released on Demos Vol. 5. During the intro of Fried, which contained a Surf Rider (The Lively Ones) tease, Joey announced that Stu had gotten too fried and had left the band, which Stu apologized for and repeated that he's "just a normal person." During the song Stu broke three of his guitar strings. After Fried, Stu asked if anyone had a guitar with more than one string, then congratulated Forte on their 500th issue, followed by Joey advertising their new CDs (likely early Anglesea copies).

jamchart_notes: Only known recording, and the first example of the band jamming out a song. Almost double the length of the studio version.

Following Ghost, an interview was conducted with the band, and then the station played Eddie Cousin. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The radio setlist published online notes that both Ghost and Eddie Cousin were played, but only Ghost was specified as having been performed live. Since no other songs were noted as live, we are unsure if this was a recording of Ghost as performed on 2/12/11 or if it was performed live, so we have chosen to note Ghost as being performed live. If anyone has any information on what was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlist@kglw.net.

In-studio performance for the RMIT student-made TV show The Temperature's Rising. Eddie Cousin and the complete performance of Trench Foot were broadcast in Series 2, Episode 1 on September 10, 2011. Trench Foot contained a false start due to Eric’s cymbal falling on Cavs. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely complete. The date is unconfirmed; KGLW.net staff used the April 25th upload date for the false start outtake of Trench Foot as the date of the performance. If anyone has any further information about this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show was performed as part of a community radio promotion for the Willoughby’s Beach EP and featured the earliest confirmed performances of Danger $$$, Black Tooth, Let It Bleed, Dead-Beat, Dustbin Fletcher, and Willoughby’s Beach. Crookedile was dedicated to The Vasco Era vocalist Sid O’Neil (and Cook Craig). Let It Bleed was introduced as “not a Rolling Stones cover or anything”. Dustbin Fletcher was introduced as being “named after our favorite Essendon veteran”, a reference to Essendon Football Club defender Dustin Fletcher. Stoned Mullet was introduced as being named after a band Stu thought was “really cool” when he was in high school. KGLW.net Staff Notes: We note these as "first confirmed performances" of most of these songs due in part to so few King Gizzard recordings from this time period. Public recordings of King Gizz from this time period are so rare that these distinctions should in no way be confused with debuts, which may have actually been much earlier. KGLW.net staff is 100% confident in "debuts" as the first time played live...unless its Satan Speeds Up, then we just have to trust what Stu says.

King Gizzard appeared as part of the Meredith Music Festival, a “dream” for the band. Elbow was "pretty different" compared to the final album recording. After Talk Talk Talk, Stu mentioned his new 12-string Stratocaster that was also featured in the Muckraker music video saying "it's sick". He mentions it again after High Hopes Low saying it was "really hard to tune". I Wanna Be Your Dog was sung to the tune of Crookedile. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show may be incomplete and out of order. Elbow was confirmed by Stu in a track-by-track for 12 Bar Bruise here, Black Tooth and Lunch Meat were reported by the linked reviews and Talk Talk Talk through I Wanna Be Your Dog were confirmed by the audio recording. The beginning of Talk Talk Talk is missing, and it is unknown if additional songs were played prior. If anyone has any information on what else may have been played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

He mentions it again after High Hopes Low saying it was "really hard to tune". I Wanna Be Your Dog was sung to the tune of Crookedile. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show may be incomplete and out of order. Elbow was confirmed by Stu in a track-by-track for 12 Bar Bruise here, Black Tooth and Lunch Meat were reported by the linked reviews and Talk Talk Talk through I Wanna Be Your Dog were confirmed by the audio recording. The beginning of Talk Talk Talk is missing, and it is unknown if additional songs were played prior. If anyone has any information on what else may have been played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show was a performance for the New Year's Eve Esplanade celebration/festival. Elbow contained different lyrics to the finished release. After Black Tooth, Joey asked if anyone from the crowd is from Deniliquin. After Talk Talk Talk, Stu mentioned his new 12-string Stratocaster that was also featured in the Muckraker music video, saying that he "loves it, but it's really hard to tune". Before Dead-Beat, Enter Sandman (Metallica) was teased.
